title: Mastering Temple and Frown Line Fillers: Safe Techniques for Resistant Cases
filename: Mastering Temple and Frown Line Fillers： Safe Techniques for Resistant Cases.en.vtt
excerpt: In this case in the upper face it's all once again led by consultation understanding what the patient is concerned by and then educating them on what is the underlying cause of that so she had frown lines that had become resistant to Toxin and so she actually needed volume undern…

segments.text: in this case in the upper face it's all once again led by consultation understanding what the patient is concerned by and then educating them on what is the underlying cause of that so she had frown lines that had become resistant to Toxin and so she actually needed volume underneath her debella something she'd had many years previously with a different technique when I directly treated her frown lines she also self-presented saying she needed Temple volume replacement but also when you look closely after correcting a temples you often see that the lateral frontal fat pad is also depleted if you want to get a smooth continuous shape along the forehead you often need to treat lateral frontal fat pad and temples together and then sometimes even the line that divides the two of them if there's a shadow running along the temple Crest you fill underneath and above that to make it one continuous smooth line so in this video you will learn how to treat a risky area in what I believe to be a safe way we use a lot of canul patience and some techniques while using the canula that

validate that it's not in a vessel I've been looking before I started treating the temple for any risks that I can avoid so with the ultrasound scanner I couldn't see an archery here and I but I always use a finger as well because I think you know just it's just layering up sometimes your probe can be angled in the wrong direction or potentially you can squash a small vessel so you can't see it on the ultrasound and then you can feel it more with your finger I always do both so I've now done that um I can see this transition where the shadow forms which is my aesthetic goal and I'm now Choosing My entry point to do the restoration of volume I like to angle in such a way as I cannot if I go too deep go into an area that I wouldn't want to so worst case scenario this is nowhere near where I'd inject but if I was to inject back here this is just over the terion of the suture point so remember there are these bones in your skull they all meet at a particular point and the terion is a weak point and it's quite thin obviously gets thinner

as you get older and you could in theory put a needle straight through that and then you'd be in introducing filler into the cranium so I always inject in a way that would be impossible because I'm now angled up towards the front tus so my angle of Entry is actually continuous if I was to go deep I'd be going into the frontal bone I'm not going to do that anyway because I don't I only need to kiss the bone and stop but it just gives me another layer of safety I've checked for vessels can't see any here so I'm now ready cleaned the skin multiple times uh so now we will enter in touch the bone which isn't as deep as you may think and then we'll do a nice long aspiration now movement after the aspirate is fine because I have a negative pressure it actually increases sensitivity to intravascular placement close off really slowly

now nothing wrong if you're on Lower risk to move the needle again slightly a millimeter here there aspe again just keep reducing the risk of a large basc cusion [Music] cuz I touched the bow and the needles a little bit blunt now even though I've only touched it once so I can hear that noise I'm not going to come in and out again little massage so this is looking good that's leveled it we haven't got an

outward curve which is not what I want a flat area but I can now see the opportunity above to improve the lateral frontal fat pad and there often the way in this area you correct one area and you see the transition more clearly elsewhere so we'll do that once we've done the other side of the temple so I have a new needle now I will feel again just in case there are any vessels aspirate give it some [Music] time slowly start to inject

[Music] little massage right so now we're just going to do a tiny bit of airbrushing here just to try and change the Contour to be a slightly more harmonious shift it is it's not exactly a distraction at all this super subtle but if we're going to make someone look fresher and younger hiding the transition points between Temple lateral forehead and frontal Eminence is one way to do it this is a lot what makeup actually does for people so um I'm going to once again feel for any blood vessels uh this is

um a deep injection if you can get to the periosteum they'll be using a canul bevel will be facing downwards and the trickiest bit is just finding your layer once you're in it's usually pretty [Music] straightforward this is an interesting place to treat just here where the corrugator inserts you often get a little Shadow caused by frowning that doesn't go away after Toxin and this can be a nice way to make someone look more relaxed very little product so we'll see if we can Edge our way into there very much relying on ease of

movement if you want to add an extra layer you can compress where the vessels might be [Music] if you just look at the reflection on the skin here there's more complexity on this side that's not treated than on this side which is just looks a bit more matte it's not actually more matte it's just spreading out the light in a more uh consistent way so that you don't see clusters of it so it already looks better um but just like makeup more than anything else it's quite interesting how different that looks with in a very subtle way so we should now get the same result here just by adding volume into this section

[Music] so the next area I just want to add a little bit of volume here is the leella so this requires a slightly different entry point because I simply cannot reach across all of it so I'm going to go in lateral to the gabella now I want to I'm often thinking about super orbital here I want to Miss super orbital go underneath them hopefully but my one of my defenses here is simply being gentle and patient so I call these the analog variables because it's impossible to

measure how much patience and makes you safer but it seems obvious to me that it would but we'll never have a randomized control trial okay moving freely aspirate compress any vessels inject while moving I'm also at 90° to any vessels which makes it far less likely that you'll be in [Music]

them so I'm just seeing a little Shadow here that I can't ignore before I move on to the rest of the face I want to just improve this little indentation here it's part of the temple so where we're injected we just need a bit more [Music]
